paper_title: Diatoms from the Colombian Amazon: some species ...

methods.description: Collection of planktonic and benthic diatom samples from various water bodies in the Colombian Amazon basin during 1993 and 1994.

steps.description: Samples for the analysis were collected during 1993 and 1994 in seven different water bodies in the Colombian Amazon basin: Tarapoto Lake, Arara Creek, Resaca Lake, Tunda Lake, belong to Solimões River (Amazon River), Quinina Lake to Iça River (Putumayo) and Taraira I Lake to Japurá River (Caquetá).
steps.searchable_summary: collect aquatic samples from specific lakes and rivers in the Colombian Amazon basin

steps.description: Horizontal and vertical tows were made with a plankton net (mesh size = 24 µm) at different depths of the photic zone
steps.searchable_summary: perform horizontal and vertical plankton net tows at different depths

steps.description: benthic diatoms were sampled by squeezing macrophytes.
steps.searchable_summary: collect benthic diatom samples by squeezing macrophytes

methods.description: Measurement of physical and chemical parameters at each sampling site.

steps.description: At each sampling site physical and chemical parameters: pH, dissolved oxygen, temperature, water transparency (Secchi disc) and conductivity were registered (Tab.1).
steps.searchable_summary: measure water quality parameters (pH, dissolved oxygen, temperature, transparency, conductivity) at sampling sites

methods.description: Fixation and cleaning of diatom samples to eliminate organic matter, followed by mounting for light and scanning electron microscopy.

steps.description: were treated to eliminate organic matter following the method described in Hasle & Fryxell (1970) and/or in a muffle furnace at 500 °C during 10 minutes.
steps.searchable_summary: clean diatom samples to remove organic matter using chemical digestion or muffle furnace
inputs: chemicals (Hasle & Fryxell method) OR muffle furnace

steps.description: Samples for light microscopy (LM) were mounted in Hyrax
steps.searchable_summary: mount cleaned diatom samples in Hyrax for light microscopy

steps.description: for scanning electron microscopy (SEM) were mounted on glass stubs
steps.searchable_summary: mount cleaned diatom samples on glass stubs for scanning electron microscopy

methods.description: Observation of prepared diatom samples using light and scanning electron microscopy.

steps.description: Observations were carried out with a Wild M20 LM
steps.searchable_summary: observe LM slides using a light microscope
observe SEM stubs using a scanning electron microscope

methods.description: Deposition of collected and prepared samples in specified collections/herbaria for archiving.

steps.description: Materials are deposited in the Colección Ficoteca Amazónica at the Universidad Nacional de Colombia (Tab.1).
steps.searchable_summary: deposit collected materials in the Colección Ficoteca Amazónica

steps.description: Unclean and cleaned subsamples and permanent slides are deposited at the Herbario of the Departamento Científico Ficología, Museo de Ciencias Naturales de La Plata.
steps.searchable_summary: deposit subsamples and permanent slides in the Herbario at Museo de Ciencias Naturales de La Plata
